name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1055.013 - Process Doppelganging via NTFS Transaction APIs
description: Detects indicators of Process Doppelganging, a fileless injection technique that abuses NTFS Transactional (TxF) operations to replace a legitimate process image with malicious code without writing to disk. Observed in APT37 (Rust-based backdoor via NtCreateTransaction/NtRollbackTransaction) and GhostPulse loader deploying Rhadamanthys, SectopRAT, Vidar. IDAT Loader used this to deploy LummaC2 into explorer.exe. Ranked #1 technique (T1055) in Picus Red Report 2026 with 30% prevalence across 326K+ malware samples.
query: process.command_line: (*CREATE_SUSPENDED*) OR process.command_line: (*NtCreateTransaction* OR *NtRollbackTransaction* OR *NtCreateSection* OR *NtCreateProcessEx* OR *CreateFileTransacted*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1055.012 - Process Ghosting - Delete-Pending File Execution
description: Detects Process Ghosting, a stealthy injection technique where attackers create a process from a file marked for deletion (delete-pending state). CherryLoader malware was observed using this technique with CreateFile + DELETE flag, NtSetInformationFile with FILE_DISPOSITION_INFORMATION, then NtCreateSection and NtCreateProcess. Picus Red Report 2026 documents this as an emerging variant of T1055 Process Injection (30% prevalence, #1 technique).
query: (process.executable: (*\\Temp\\* OR *\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\* OR *\\Downloads\\*) AND process.command_line: (*NtSetInformationFile* OR *NtCreateSection* OR *DELETE_PENDING*))
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1055.003 - Waiting Thread Hijacking - Dormant Thread Context Modification
description: Detects Waiting Thread Hijacking, a 2025-discovered evolution of thread execution hijacking where adversaries target existing threads in a waiting state rather than creating new suspended threads. The attacker allocates memory inside a legitimate process, writes a payload, and modifies the dormant thread's context so the next wake event redirects execution to attacker-controlled code. Used by banking Trojans and credential-stealers. Also detects Threadless Operations.
query: process where event.action == "access" and process.Ext.api.name in ("SetThreadContext", "NtSetContextThread", "NtQueueApcThread") and process.executable : ("*.exe") and Target.process.executable : ("*\\explorer.exe", "*\\svchost.exe", "*\\RuntimeBroker.exe", "*\\sihost.exe", "*\\taskhostw.exe") and not process.executable : ("*\\Windows\\System32\\*", "*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "*\\Program Files\\*", "*\\Program Files (x86)\\*")
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1497 - Sandbox Evasion - VM Artifact and Mouse Trigonometry Checks
description: Detects processes performing sandbox evasion by checking for VM artifacts or analyzing mouse movement patterns using trigonometric calculations. LummaC2 v4.0 calculates the Euclidean distance and angles of cursor paths to distinguish human users from automated sandboxes. T1497 surged to #4 in Picus Red Report 2026.
query: process.command_line: (*GetCursorPos* OR *GetTickCount* OR *QueryPerformanceCounter*) OR process.command_line: (*VBOX* OR *VMWARE* OR *VIRTUAL* OR *QEMU* OR *vmtoolsd* OR *vboxservice*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1497.001 - VM/Sandbox Detection via Registry Queries
description: Detects processes querying registry keys commonly used for virtualization and sandbox environment detection. T1497 surged to rank #4 in Picus Red Report 2026. Modern malware including LummaC2 and Blitz performs extensive environment fingerprinting before detonating.
query: registry.path: (*SOFTWARE\\VMware*Inc* OR *SOFTWARE\\Oracle\\VirtualBox* OR *Services\\VBoxGuest* OR *Services\\vmci* OR *Services\\vmhgfs* OR *Virtual*Machine\\Guest\\Parameters* OR *Enum\\PCI\\VEN_15AD* OR *Enum\\PCI\\VEN_80EE*) AND NOT process.executable: (*\\VMware\\* OR *\\VirtualBox\\*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1071 - Cloud API C2 Communication (OpenAI/AWS/Azure)
description: Detects suspicious outbound DNS queries to cloud API endpoints commonly abused as covert C2 channels by non-browser processes. SesameOp backdoor used OpenAI Assistants API as C2 relay. LameHug and MalTerminal also abused cloud APIs. Storm-0501 queried AWS Secrets Manager to harvest credentials. IOCONTROL (Cyber Av3ngers) used application layer protocols for C2. Picus Red Report 2026 #5 technique.
query: dns.question.name: (*api.openai.com* OR *lambda-url.on.aws* OR *execute-api.amazonaws.com* OR *secretsmanager.amazonaws.com* OR *vault.azure.net* OR *cloudfunctions.net* OR *run.app* OR *azurewebsites.net*) AND NOT process.executable: (*\\chrome.exe OR *\\msedge.exe OR *\\firefox.exe OR *\\brave.exe OR *\\opera.exe OR *\\iexplore.exe OR *\\Python* OR *\\node.exe OR *\\code.exe OR *\\Visual*Studio* OR *\\dotnet.exe)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1036.002 - RTLO Character in Filename (Masquerading)
description: Detects the use of Right-to-Left Override (RTLO/RLO) Unicode character (U+202E) in filenames to disguise executable files as benign document types. Ferocious Kitten campaign used RTLO to flip file extensions, making a malicious .exe appear as .pdf or image file. T1036 Masquerading rose to #6 in Picus Red Report 2026 (16.59% prevalence).
query: file.name: (*‮* OR *‎* OR *‏*) OR process.command_line: (*‮* OR *‎* OR *‏*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1036.001 - System Process Execution from Non-Standard Path (Masquerading)
description: Detects execution of binaries named after legitimate Windows system processes but running from non-standard paths. Mustang Panda deployed malware with invalid digital signatures that fooled superficial checks. Warp Panda used BRICKSTORM and Junction malware. T1036 Masquerading ranked #6 in Picus Red Report 2026.
query: (process.name: (svchost.exe OR csrss.exe OR lsass.exe OR services.exe OR smss.exe OR winlogon.exe OR spoolsv.exe OR wininit.exe OR taskhost.exe OR RuntimeBroker.exe OR update.exe)) AND NOT process.executable: (C\:\\Windows\\System32\\* OR C\:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\* OR C\:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1547 - Linux XDG Autostart and Systemd User Service Persistence
description: Detects creation of .desktop files in XDG autostart directories or systemd user services on Linux, persistence techniques used by EtherRAT and APT36/Transparent Tribe's swcbc malware. T1547 Boot or Logon Autostart Execution rose to #7 in Picus Red Report 2026.
query: (file.path: (*/.config/autostart/*.desktop OR */.local/share/autostart/*.desktop OR */.config/systemd/user/*.service)) AND NOT process.executable: (*/apt* OR */dpkg* OR */rpm* OR */yum* OR */dnf* OR */pacman*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1562.001 - Impair Defenses - Defender Disable and Exclusion Abuse
description: Detects attempts to disable Windows Defender via SystemSettingsAdminFlows.exe abuse, Add-MpPreference exclusion injection, AMSI bypass, or registry manipulation. Deadlock ransomware abused SystemSettingsAdminFlows.exe. ValleyRAT injects Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath. Used by Mustang Panda, Crypto24, Mimic, Remcos RAT, LockBit, RansomHub, Medusa. T1562 ranked #8 in Picus Red Report 2026 (14.18%).
query: (process.executable: *SystemSettingsAdminFlows.exe AND process.command_line: (*DisableRealtimeMonitoring* OR *DisableAntiSpyware* OR *DisableIOAVProtection*)) OR (process.command_line: (*Add-MpPreference* AND *-ExclusionPath*)) OR (process.command_line: (*DisableRealtimeMonitoring* OR *DisableAntiSpyware* OR *DisableBehaviorMonitoring* OR *DisableIOAVProtection* OR *DisableOnAccessProtection*) AND process.command_line: (*reg*add* OR *Set-ItemProperty* OR *New-ItemProperty*)) OR process.command_line: (*AmsiInitFailed* OR *AmsiScanBuffer*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1562.001 - EDR Blinding via Vulnerable Driver Loading (BYOVD)
description: Detects loading of known-vulnerable kernel drivers used to blind EDR sensors by removing kernel callbacks. RealBlindingEDR tools surgically remove kernel callbacks. SplatCloak (Mustang Panda) and ransomware families (Deadlock, Crypto24, Mimic, LockBit, RansomHub, Medusa) use BYOVD as standard first step. Also detects unsigned drivers loaded from user-writable paths.
query: driver.name: (RTCore64.sys OR DBUtil_2_3.sys OR gdrv.sys OR PROCEXP152.sys OR ene.sys OR WinRing0.sys OR speedfan.sys OR phymem64.sys OR AsmMap64.sys) OR (file.path: (*\\Temp\\*.sys OR *\\AppData\\*.sys OR *\\Downloads\\*.sys OR *\\ProgramData\\*.sys) AND event.category: driver)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1219 - VS Code Remote Tunnel and Portable Remote Access Tool Abuse
description: Detects the use of Visual Studio Code Remote Tunnels (code.exe tunnel) or portable remote access tools from suspicious paths as persistence mechanisms. Picus Red Report 2026 (#9 technique, T1219) documents attackers weaponizing VS Code Tunnels, AnyDesk, TeamViewer. DPRK operatives also used IPKVM hardware. Chaos, Akira, and DeadLock ransomware families also abuse remote access tools.
query: (process.executable: (*\\code.exe OR *\\code-insiders.exe) AND process.command_line: *tunnel*) OR (process.executable: (*\\code.exe) AND process.command_line: (*tunnel*--accept-server-license-terms* OR *tunnel*user*login* OR *tunnel*service*install*)) OR (process.executable: (*\\Temp\\* OR *\\AppData\\* OR *\\Downloads\\* OR *\\ProgramData\\*) AND process.name: (AnyDesk.exe OR TeamViewer.exe OR ScreenConnect.exe OR rustdesk.exe))
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1486 - Ransomware Pre-Encryption Preparation (VSS/Backup Destruction)
description: Detects the full ransomware preparation chain: VSS shadow deletion, VSS service disabling, boot recovery disabling, ransom wallpaper setting, and backup catalog deletion. Picus Red Report 2026 (#10 technique, T1486) documents Qilin, RansomHub, DragonForce, LockBit 3.0, Medusa, Lynx, Anubis, Nefilim using hybrid encryption. Sandworm's ZEROLOT/Sting/PathWiper also covered.
query: process.command_line: (*vssadmin*Delete*Shadows* OR *wmic*shadowcopy*delete*) OR process.command_line: (*ChangeStartMode*Disabled* OR *sc*config*vss*start*disabled*) OR (process.name: bcdedit.exe AND process.command_line: (*recoveryenabled*No* OR *bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*)) OR (process.command_line: (*reg*add* AND *Wallpaper* AND *ProgramData*)) OR process.command_line: (*wbadmin*delete*catalog* OR *wbadmin*delete*systemstatebackup*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1555.003 - Browser Credential Store Access by Non-Browser Process
description: Detects non-browser processes accessing browser credential storage files (Login Data, Cookies, Local State, key4.db, logins.json). T1555 ranked #3 in Picus Red Report 2026 (23% prevalence, 254K+ samples). SantaStealer bypassed Chrome AppBound encryption. Also Meduza Stealer, BeaverTail, Shai-Hulud 2.0, Makop ransomware. Earth Ammit, UNC3944, Storm-0501 APTs.
query: (file.path: (*\\Google\\Chrome\\User*Data\\Default\\Login*Data* OR *\\Google\\Chrome\\User*Data\\Default\\Cookies* OR *\\Google\\Chrome\\User*Data\\Local*State* OR *\\Google\\Chrome\\User*Data\\Default\\Web*Data* OR *\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User*Data\\Default\\Login*Data* OR *\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User*Data\\Default\\Cookies* OR *\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User*Data\\Local*State* OR *\\BraveSoftware\\Brave-Browser\\User*Data\\Default\\Login*Data*) OR (file.path: *\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\* AND file.name: (logins.json OR key4.db OR cookies.sqlite))) AND NOT process.executable: (*\\chrome.exe OR *\\msedge.exe OR *\\firefox.exe OR *\\brave.exe) AND NOT process.executable: (C\:\\Windows\\System32\\* OR C\:\\Program*Files\\* OR C\:\\Program*Files*(x86)\\*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] T1059.001 - PowerShell In-Memory Download and Execute
description: Detects PowerShell one-liners that download and execute payloads in memory using IEX/DownloadString, Base64 encoded commands, hidden window + no-profile combos, or ScriptBlock::Create. DragonForce used powershell -nop -w hidden -c IEX. HellCat ransomware used multi-stage ScriptBlock chains. T1059 #2 technique in Picus Red Report 2026 (27% prevalence).
query: (process.command_line: (*IEX* OR *Invoke-Expression* OR *iex*) AND process.command_line: (*DownloadString* OR *DownloadData* OR *Invoke-WebRequest* OR *iwr* OR *Net.WebClient* OR *WebClient*)) OR (process.command_line: (*FromBase64String* OR *-EncodedCommand* OR *-enc* OR *-ec*) AND process.command_line: (*Invoke-Expression* OR *IEX* OR *iex*)) OR (process.command_line: (*powershell*) AND process.command_line: (*-w*hidden*) AND process.command_line: (*-nop*)) OR (process.command_line: (*ScriptBlock* AND *Create* AND *iwr*))
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] Mustang Panda - LSASS Credential Dump via Mimikatz from Temp
description: Detects Mustang Panda's documented credential dumping pattern using Mimikatz from %TMP% or user-writable directories to harvest LSASS memory. Picus Red Report 2026 documents the exact command: mimikatz.exe privilege::debug sekurlsa::logonPasswords exit. Mustang Panda also uses SplatCloak for EDR evasion, Paklog/Corklog keyloggers.
query: (process.executable: (*\\Temp\\* OR *\\AppData\\* OR *\\ProgramData\\* OR *\\Downloads\\*) AND process.command_line: (*sekurlsa\:\:logonPasswords* OR *privilege\:\:debug* OR *lsadump\:\:sam* OR *lsadump\:\:dcsync* OR *sekurlsa*))
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] UNC3886 - Junos OS Shell History Clearing and Code Injection
description: Detects UNC3886 (China-nexus) TTPs targeting Juniper Junos OS routers with TINYSHELL backdoor variants. The group uses export HISTFILE='', unset HISTFILE, history -c, and rm -f shell history files. Also uses mkfifo and dd for code injection via named pipes to bypass Veriexec file integrity.
query: process.command_line: (*export*HISTFILE* OR *unset*HISTFILE* OR *history*-c* OR *rm*-f*sh_history* OR *rm*-f*bash_history*) OR (process.command_line: (*mkfifo*) AND process.command_line: (*dd*)) OR process.command_line: (*/proc/*/mem*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] UNC3944 - ESXi Hypervisor CLI Abuse and VMDK Manipulation
description: Detects UNC3944 (Scattered Spider) TTPs targeting VMware ESXi hypervisor. After social engineering help desk agents, UNC3944 enables SSH, resets root passwords, disables execInstalledOnly, powers off VMs, detaches DC VMDKs to extract NTDS.dit offline, then deploys ransomware via ESXi shell.
query: process.command_line: (*vim-cmd*hostsvc*enable_ssh* OR *esxcli*system*account*set* OR *esxcli*system*settings*advanced*set*) OR process.command_line: (*execInstalledOnly*) OR process.command_line: (*vim-cmd*vmsvc*power.off* OR *esxcli*vm*process*kill*) OR process.command_line: (*vmkfstools* OR *vmdk*) OR (process.command_line: (*nohup*/tmp/* OR *chmod*+x*/tmp/*) AND process.command_line: (*.vmdk* OR *.vmx* OR *datastore*))
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] DPRK Laptop Farm - IPKVM Hardware Device Indicators
description: Detects registry indicators of IPKVM devices (PiKVM, TinyPilot, JetKVM) used by DPRK operatives for BIOS-level remote access to corporate laptop farms. These hardware devices operate below the OS, rendering EDR blind. T1219.003 Remote Access Hardware - Picus Red Report 2026.
query: registry.path: (*USB\\VID_1D6B* OR *USB\\VID_534D* OR *PiKVM* OR *TinyPilot* OR *JetKVM*)
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] CABINETRAT - Scheduled Task Persistence and Admin SID Check
description: Detects CABINETRAT malware TTPs: 12-hour scheduled tasks from fake Microsoft Office paths, admin privilege checks via whoami/SID S-1-5-32-544, and Run registry key persistence disguised as ChromeUpdater. Targets South Asia. Picus Red Report 2026.
query: (process.name: schtasks.exe AND process.command_line: (*\\Microsoft\\Office\\* OR *\\Microsoft*Office\\*)) OR (process.command_line: (*schtasks* AND *hourly* AND */mo*12*)) OR (process.command_line: (*whoami*/groups*) AND process.command_line: (*S-1-5-32-544*)) OR (process.command_line: (*reg*add* AND *CurrentVersion\\Run* AND *ChromeUpdater*))
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026

name: [Picus Red Report 2026] MuddyWater - SMB/WMI Remote Execution for Lateral Movement
description: Detects MuddyWater (Iran-nexus) lateral movement using Invoke-SMBExec with NTLM hashes, wmic /node: process call create for remote PowerShell execution, and Invoke-Command -ComputerName for hands-off lateral spread. Picus Red Report 2026 T1059.
query: process.command_line: (*Invoke-SMBExec* OR *Invoke-WMIExec* OR *Invoke-TheHash*) OR (process.command_line: (*wmic*) AND process.command_line: (*/node\:*) AND process.command_line: (*process*call*create*)) OR (process.command_line: (*Invoke-Command*) AND process.command_line: (*-ComputerName*) AND process.command_line: (*-ScriptBlock*))
author: Loaded Potato - Picus Red Report 2026
